<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user encountered an authorization error while creating a change of address case. The system rejected their request because the 'ReceptionOfficer' role is not permitted to list these cases.

The user appears to be logged in as a ReceptionOfficer rather than a RegistrationOfficer. The authorization rules likely need updating to allow RegistrationOfficer access to change of address cases.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The ReceptionOfficer role can create change of address cases but cannot list them. This permission gap likely causes the unauthorized access error.

The user may have been logged in as `ReceptionOfficer` rather than `PopulationOfficer`. Alternatively, a role switching bug could have caused the wrong role to persist. Another possibility is that the user reached the change-of-address page through an incorrect navigation path.

The reception officer may be redirected to the change-of-address list page after creating a case. That redirect would trigger the unauthorized access error.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is located on the NewRegistrationCasePage.

The `/change-of-address` page restricts access to `PopulationOfficer` only. `ReceptionOfficer` roles are allowed to create change of address cases. This mismatch causes the unauthorized access error.

The user is a ReceptionOfficer, not a PopulationOfficer. The RegistrationRoleGate may redirect before OnInitializedAsync runs. The fix options are being evaluated.

Reception officers should not be redirected to the change-of-address list page. They likely need a direct dialog or a dedicated reception page instead. Allowing them to list cases would violate existing tests. The next step is to examine how the registration flow handles birth declarations for reception officers.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The bug is located in `RegistrationRoleGate.razor`.

The `@if` check in `RegistrationRoleGate.razor` blocks child content for unauthorized roles. `ChangeOfAddressCaseList.razor` only allows `PopulationOfficer`. The `NavigateTo` redirect is asynchronous and does not block rendering. This causes the child component to render without the role gate allowing it.

The `@code` block belongs to the parent page component, not the child content inside the role gate. Because `ChangeOfAddressCaseList` is the root component, its `OnInitializedAsync` runs regardless of whether the child page renders. This explains why the API call executes even though the user lacks permission.

The page's `OnInitializedAsync` unconditionally calls `ReloadAsync()`, which triggers the API request before the role gate can block it. The unauthorized access error therefore occurs during initialization, while the asynchronous redirect from the gate is still pending.

The user described themselves as a "registration officer," yet the error shows a "ReceptionOfficer" role. This mismatch suggests the user may have been logged in under the wrong role, or the new-case creation flow may have restored incorrect session data.

The user likely confused the Population Officer role with the registration officer role. A true Population Officer would not trigger this error, because the new-case page redirects them to the cases list. The error instead indicates the user was logged in as a ReceptionOfficer, or the role detection is malfunctioning. Either way, the underlying bugs in the role gate and initialization logic still need fixing.

The new-case page redirects ReceptionOfficer to the change-of-address list, but that list is restricted to PopulationOfficer. A reception-friendly flow is needed for reception officers who create cases.

After a reception officer opens a case, they cannot view it either. The current permissions block them from both listing and viewing change of address cases.

The change of address dialog navigates to the case detail page after creation. That page also requires PopulationOfficer permission. The intended flow for a reception officer should instead show a local success card, similar to how birth declaration cases work on the new-case page. The OpenChangeOfAddressCase handler needs to follow the same direct-open-and-success pattern rather than redirecting to list or detail views.

The passport case page likely shares the same broken redirect pattern. The fix plan is to handle change of address inline on the new-case page for reception officers, and to guard ChangeOfAddressCaseList so it checks role before calling ReloadAsync.

Also considering a fix in `RegistrationRoleGate` itself, perhaps by moving API calls to `OnAfterRender` or preventing unauthorized pages from invoking APIs at all. The identity-documents page will be checked next to see how it handles `open=new` for reception officers.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The reception flow routes users to `/change-of-address?open=new` from `NewRegistrationCasePage`. This triggers the unauthorized access exception.

The page crashes during initialization because `ReloadAsync()` requires `PopulationOfficer` role, but the user has `ReceptionOfficer`. `RegistrationRoleGate` redirects too late to prevent the exception. A `PopulationOfficer` would not hit this issue, since they can list change of address cases.

The user likely meant "reception officer" by "registration officer." Another possibility is that the URL retained a previous officer ID after a role switch. I will inspect `DemoOfficerPersistence` and `RoleSwitcher` to verify how the officer ID persists across navigation.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Marie is a PopulationOfficer in the Population department.

The user likely has a ReceptionOfficer role. The fix should let reception officers create change-of-address cases directly on the new-case page instead of routing to the list page. The case list pages should guard their API calls so unauthorized roles cannot trigger requests. Birth declarations already handle reception intake this way.

The new-case page will open a dialog via IDialogService. A success confirmation will display the created case ID with visit reason ChangeOfAddress. _**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The cause of the unauthorized access error is now clear. A fix is being identified.

The fix targets three components. NewRegistrationCasePage.razor will open the dialog inline instead of navigating. ChangeOfAddressCaseList.razor will skip reloads and early returns for unauthorized roles. DocumentRequestCaseList.razor will receive the same treatment.

The NewRegistrationCasePage fix will inject IDialogService and open the change of address dialog inline. After the dialog closes, the page will display a success confirmation rather than navigating to a detail page. This matches the BirthDeclaration pattern.

The ChangeOfAddressCaseList guard will return early in OnInitializedAsync if the current officer is not a PopulationOfficer. The DocumentRequestCaseList will receive the identical check. After the inline dialog closes successfully, the page will display the new case ID and a hand-off message without navigating to the detail page.

A page-level authorization guard is preferred over a RegistrationRoleGate redirect, since the page code still executes during render. Inline role checks are kept simple and consistent with the existing gate logic. Integration and end-to-end tests for the reception officer change of address flow will also be added.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The RegistrationCaseList page already contains guards for ReceptionOfficer and PoliceClerk roles before loading.

ChangeOfAddressCaseList lacks the role guards present in RegistrationCaseList. The fix requires adding those guards to ChangeOfAddressCaseList, DocumentRequestCaseList, BirthDeclarationCaseList, and their detail pages. NewRegistrationCasePage should open dialogs inline instead of navigating to the list. The PassportRenewal, DocumentRequest, and identity document flows on that page need similar inline fixes.

The fix will use inline role checks rather than a shared helper. NewRegistrationCasePage will inject IDialogService and add usings for the change-of-address and identity-document dialog components. It will then open those dialogs inline based on the visit reason, returning early to avoid navigation.

NewRegistrationCasePage will open the change-of-address dialog inline and display a success snackbar upon creation. The list pages will check for PopulationOfficer role at the start of OnInitializedAsync and exit early if unauthorized. Detail pages will apply the same guard before reloading the case.

BirthDeclarationCaseList also lacks a role guard. The birth declaration flow redirects reception officers to the API directly, while population officers navigate through RegistrationCaseList. I will implement the fixes and add an end-to-end test for reception officers creating change of address cases from the new-case page. I will also add an integration test verifying that reception officers can open cases but cannot list them. I am reviewing RegistrationTestHelpers for existing test patterns.
</details></think>

Identified the issue: reception officers are sent to `/change-of-address?open=new`, but the list page loads cases before the role gate runs. Fixing the reception intake flow and adding role guards on those pages.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Integration tests pass. I will explain what caused the error and how it was fixed.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

The error came from a **role mismatch in the intake flow**, not from population officers lacking access.

## What happened

The stack trace shows role **`ReceptionOfficer`** (Jean at the reception desk), not **`PopulationOfficer`** (Marie/Anne in the Population Department — the role that manages change-of-address cases).

When you picked **“Change of address”** on the **New case** page (`/registration/new-case`), the app redirected to `/change-of-address?open=new`. That page immediately called the list API in `OnInitializedAsync`, but only population officers may list cases. Reception officers may **create** cases, not browse the list — so the page crashed before the role gate could redirect.

## What I fixed

1. **Reception intake stays on the new-case page** — change-of-address and passport renewal now open their search dialogs inline and show a “Case created” confirmation (same pattern as birth declarations), instead of navigating to population-only list pages.

2. **Role guards on list/detail pages** — change-of-address and identity-document pages skip API calls when the current role isn’t allowed, so direct URL access redirects cleanly instead of throwing.

## How to use it

| Role | How to create a change of address |
|------|-----------------------------------|
| **Reception** (Jean) | **New case** → visit reason “Change of address” → search NR → confirm |
| **Population** (Marie/Anne) | Sidebar **Change of address** → **New change of address** |

If you’re doing full case management (list, edit, confirm), switch to a **Population Officer** via the role switcher in the top bar.

Reload the app and try again — the reception flow should open the NR search dialog and show a success card without crashing.
